home.benefit.remote.title.message: Start a new thread from iPhone

home.benefit.remote.body.message: While connected on the same LAN, you can start a new thread from the iPhone side.

home.benefit.setup.title.message: Pairing finishes in a few steps

home.benefit.setup.body.message: Open the companion app on Mac, show the bridge QR, and import it on iPhone to add a Host.

home.benefit.context.body.message: Threads, branches, skills, and commands stay grouped around each Project so workspace context is easier to keep.

home.step.bridge.body.message: Prepare the pairing QR and the Project registry in the companion app.

home.step.pair.body.message: Import with QR or manual code, then wait for bridge validation and Project catalog collection.

home.step.resume.title.message: Open a Project and start a new thread

home.step.resume.body.message: Open a Project and start a new thread, skills, or exec actions from Composer.

home.scenario.commute.title.message: You want to check things from your hand on the same network

home.scenario.commute.body.message: Without sitting back at the Mac, you can still check state from iPhone while both devices are on the same LAN.

home.scenario.away.title.message: You want to start a new thread from iPhone

home.scenario.away.body.message: This is suited to starting a new thread on iPhone, not to live-carrying an in-progress PC thread.

home.scenario.split.title.message: You want setup on Mac and control on iPhone

home.scenario.split.body.message: Keep bridge, logs, and Project management on Mac while daily interaction happens on iPhone.

home.scenario.codexApp.title.message: You want to use it together with Codex App

home.scenario.codexApp.body.message: Keep the normal Codex App flow on Mac while using the iPhone as a lighter place to check things and start new threads.

home.guide.iphone.body.message: The entry point for people who want Host setup, Projects, Threads, and Composer explained first.

home.guide.mac.body.message: The entry point for people who want the companion app, bridge, QR, and logs explained first.

home.guide.shared.body.message: A dedicated section for topics that cross the boundary between iPhone and Mac.

home.guide.reference.body.message: The place for cross-cutting information such as release policy and localization.

home.meta.title.message: Control Codex on your Mac from your iPhone

home.meta.description.message: CodexPocket is a same-LAN iPhone companion for the Codex environment running on your Mac.

home.hero.title.message: Use the Codex running on your Mac lightly from your iPhone.

home.hero.lead.message: CodexPocket lets you keep the Codex runtime on Mac while bringing Host, Project, Thread, and Composer controls onto the iPhone. Today it assumes the same LAN, and it cannot yet live-carry a PC thread as-is. Instead, it already gives you a path for starting new threads from iPhone and works especially well alongside Codex App.

home.hero.meta.message: The current stable release is {releaseVersion}. Today it is same-LAN only, but the plan is to move closer to real-time synchronization over time.

home.hero.note.sync.message: Live carry-over is not yet supported

home.visual.title.message: Control on iPhone, execution on Mac. It feels lighter because the roles stay separate.

home.visual.body.message: The companion app and bridge keep the source of truth on Mac, while the iPhone becomes the fast UI for opening Projects and Threads.

home.visual.action.value.message: Start a new thread from Composer

home.visual.panel.project.body.message: The entry point for threads and branches

home.visual.panel.composer.body.message: The entry point for follow-ups, skills, and exec

home.limitations.lan.title.message: Access is limited to the same LAN

home.limitations.lan.body.message: Right now, both the Mac and the iPhone need to be on the same network.

home.limitations.realtime.title.message: You cannot yet live-carry a PC thread onto iPhone

home.limitations.realtime.body.message: An in-progress thread on the PC cannot yet be brought over to the iPhone as if it were real-time synced.

home.limitations.future.title.message: The product is planned to move closer to real-time sync

home.limitations.future.body.message: There are limits today, but the plan is to move toward an experience that feels closer to real-time synchronization.
